payload.answer: The proposed update is too large and atomized for durable memory as-is (500 separate cards from CSV rows). Please choose how to proceed.
repair_options: Do not save the 500 row-level memory cards; instead provide a concise summary of the CSV and propose a small number of durable aggregate memories.
Ask the user which specific rows or fields are important enough to remember before saving anything.
Group related rows into a smaller set of thematic memories, preserving source context without creating one memory per row.
Keep the CSV content external and save only a pointer/reference to where it lives, plus a brief description of what it contains.
If the user explicitly needs row-level recall, ask for confirmation and scope limits before considering any granular memory creation.
